title: chenopodium prostratum subsp. karoi
intro: Chenopodium prostratum subsp. karoi, also known as the narrow-leaved glasswort or Karoi's saltwort, is a type of succulent plant native to the southwestern parts of Western Australia. It possesses medicinal properties that have been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and skin conditions due to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Chenopodium prostratum subsp. karoi include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions or internally to treat digestive issues and other health problems.
